posts.title: Alpha 2 Fuel Crit Multiplier Incident
posts.description: During Alpha 2, for a short period of time, a bug was reported concerning the fuel crit multiplier.
posts.author: PCW Bug Hunters

posts.title: Info From Developers
posts.description: In this post, we’re collecting publicly shared information from the developers of World of Tanks: HEAT, gathered through casual conversations in the official Discord server.
posts.author: PCW Bug Hunters

posts.title: Developer Guide Images
posts.description: During the latest playtest, we discovered a series of images within the game files that appear to showcase specific development techniques.
posts.author: PCW Bug Hunters

posts.title: Mysterious Engine Picture
posts.description: An intriguing image of what appears to be the Pratt & Whitney J58 engine was found in the game files, leading to speculation about its connection to the in-game engine design of Raketa
posts.author: PCW Bug Hunters

posts.title: Potential Campaign Mode
posts.description: While examining the game files, two videos were found. These videos align perfectly when placed end to end, suggesting they may serve as an intro or cinematic sequence.
posts.author: PCW Bug Hunters

posts.title: Hunting For Potential Hidden Servers
posts.description: A look at the main server addresses used by the game, including a potential private dev-only server. Odd results from a recent port scan reveal only one active port.
posts.author: PCW Bug Hunters

posts.title: Unlocking the Camera
posts.description: While utilizing the same tricks to add artificial network latency and cause desyncs we successfully managed to unlock the camera for about 5-10 seconds.
posts.author: PCW Bug Hunters

posts.title: Exploring the Main Menu Garage
posts.description: After conducting some tests with HSTV-L inside the firing range, we've discovered that the room showcasing tanks in the main menu is located beneath the firing range map.
posts.author: PCW Bug Hunters

posts.title: Titan Dummies Respawn Glitch
posts.description: In version 0.9.8, an intriguing glitch has emerged involving the Titan dummies in the firing range. After being destroyed, these dummies respawn at the normal player spawn point.
posts.author: PCW Bug Hunters

posts.title: Animation and Movement Desync
posts.description: Using network latency tweaks and virtual machines, we’re uncovering how World of Tanks: HEAT triggers animations and exploring ways to experiment with in-game movement.
posts.author: PCW Bug Hunters

posts.title: Discovery of CoreX
posts.description: In this post, we uncover evidence suggesting that World of Tanks: HEAT may be powered by an upgraded version of Wargaming's Core engine, known as CoreX.
posts.author: PCW Bug Hunters

posts.title: Wierd Pattern Images
posts.description: In the game files, two images were found displaying some kind of "patterns", one with an "X" drawn over it and the other marked with a green shield.
posts.author: PCW Bug Hunters

posts.title: The Three Badges
posts.description: During the Open Alpha of February 2024, three badges were discovered in the game files: Bravo, PKS, and Poachers. These could potentially be linked to the game's three Roles.
posts.author: PCW Bug Hunters

posts.title: Tank Module Locations
posts.description: In this post, we delve into the critical modules of various tanks and their effects when damaged. Each module, from the ammo rack to the engine, plays a vital role in your tank's performance.
posts.author: PCW Bug Hunters

posts.title: Damage Calculation
posts.description: In World of Tanks: HEAT, damage depends on several key mechanics and because of this, we’ll break it down in two main sections: Critical Damage Calculation and Critical Damage Registration
posts.author: PCW Bug Hunters

posts.title: Who is PCW Bug Hunters?
posts.description: In this post we aim to answer some common questions about the PCW Bug Hunters group and our goals as a team with World of Tanks: HEAT.
posts.author: PCW Bug Hunters
